programme.title: Roadkill Garage
programme.desc: The legendary '68 Dodge Charger General Mayhem gets stripped of its 707-hp Hellcat engine and all the related electronics; Freiburger and Dulcich vow to take it from the dragstrip setup back to its motorhome 440-powered V8 and gravel-chucking roots.

programme.title: Roadkill Garage
programme.desc: The COVID-350-powered, six-speed 1956 Chevy Bel Air may sport 500 hp, but is far from perfection; Freiburger and Dulcich bring it closer to reaching their goals with some weight reduction, suspension modifications and a new 9-inch rear end.

programme.title: Roadkill Garage
programme.desc: The '56 Chevy Field Car gets ready for the drag strip.

programme.title: Roadkill Garage
programme.desc: Freiburger and Dulcich hit the road in a '66 Chevy Chevelle purchased sight unseen in an attempt to score two new project cars. The guys fix and tune their way from Washington to Arizona to rescue a dirt-cheap junkyard '75 Dodge Dart.

programme.title: Roadkill Garage
programme.desc: Freiburger and Dulcich give some much-needed love to the '69 Chevy Crusher Impala by way of a new 9-inch rear and a suspension overhaul. They also fix a major throttle issue that unlocks the 498 big block before hitting the road for monster burnouts.

programme.title: Roadkill Garage
programme.desc: Freiburger and Dulcich tune up the supercharged big block in the '69 Crusher Impala at Westech's dyno before hitting the road and the dragstrip. The guys run the Impala's fastest time in the quarter mile, but discover some new Roadkill-level issues.

programme.title: Roadkill Garage
programme.desc: Steve Dulcich and Wiley Stevens up the cam game on the Off-Road Challenger, while also seeing if Wiley's '06 Subaru can be made into a rally-capable daily driver with suspension and airflow upgrades.

programme.title: Roadkill Garage
programme.desc: Freibuger and Dulcich can't pass up buying a '41 Ford former race car that last saw action in some road races back in the late '90s; with tuning, a carburetor rebuild, exhaust modifications and some love, the guys get the Ford back on the open road.

programme.title: Roadkill Garage
programme.desc: Wiley Stevens and Chris Birdsong join Dulcich to bring new life to the car with a shifter upgrade, new clutch and a near-full rear quarter panel replacement.

programme.title: Roadkill Garage
programme.desc: Recently, while the guys were thrashing the Crusher Impala, Freiburger spotted a 440-swapped '68 Dart he couldn't pass up. Now, the guys pick up the Dart and take it home to give it an impressive engine bay makeover and horsepower-adding tune-up.

programme.title: Roadkill Garage
programme.desc: Freiburger and Dulcich revive the 318ci V-8-powered '66 Dodge D100 step-side truck from Roadkill in order to dig a buried '60 Ford Ranchero out of the backyard.

programme.title: Roadkill Garage
programme.desc: Freiburger and Dulcich bring a '60 Ford Ranchero back to life after rescuing it from over 40 years of neglect; with a fresh 283 power transplant ahead of the Muncie four-speed, the guys overhaul the entire drivetrain.

programme.title: Roadkill Garage
programme.desc: Freiburger and Dulcich troubleshoot transmission issues and more in the 1969 Chevy Impala that's powered by a supercharged 498ci big-block V8, then take it back to the track to see if they can finally get their 10-second time slip.

programme.title: Roadkill Garage
programme.desc: Dulcich enlists Wiley Stevens and Chris Birdsong to transplant a third-generation Hemi into the 1974 Dodge D100, also known as the Mopar Muscle Truck; after issues hold up progress, the guys clean up a new project 1974 Dodge Charger.

programme.title: Roadkill Garage
programme.desc: Freiburger and Dulcich attempt to get a '67 Jeep Wagoneer that languished for 31 years back on the road from Yakima, Washington; they find Dulcich's dream '64 Cadillac DeVille convertible in the process.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: Dave and the crew contend with a leaky fuel line and a tricky roof build on a 1934 Dodge; a 1967 Impala requires an exhaust upgrade; Dave looks at a 1948 Ford pickup.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: A flesh wound turns the repair of a 1953 Ford pickup into a bloody mess; a 1969 Chevrolet Camaro needs a new suspension; a 1965 Pontiac GTO rolls into the shop.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: Former professional football tight end Chad Lewis desires customizations for his Audi R8 Spyder; a 1978 Trans Am needs tubs and wheels; a 1970 Dodge Challenger excites Kevin.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The shop readies for an important outdoor show; Dave's plans for a 1927 Ford Zipper do not go as expected; the crew recalls earlier work on a 1937 Chevy.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: Dave hopes to transform an outdated 1953 Chevy into a modern hot rod; a 1934 Ford coupe "Kraken" needs an air ride refresh; a 1965 Ford Galaxie is taken for a major burn.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: A 1933 Ford Tudor arrives for a challenging paint match, as well as interior and exterior refreshes; a 1970 Dodge Challenger is restored to its Mopar glory; Dave remembers a 1955 Chevrolet Bel Air.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The Kindig crew attempts to create a 1950-style Chevrolet Fleetside, a truck model that does not exist.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: Dave has his hands full trying to locate and build a 1959 Buick Invicta; twin 2014 Audis looking for some styling cues.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The Invicta build finally comes to a show-ready end after many challenges; Dave gets down and dirty on a 1970 Mustang that comes in to get its driveline unbundled.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The Kindig crew contends with a problem-ridden 1932 roadster and a 1940 coupe; Dave's dune buggy mysteriously vanishes.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The crew ups its game to modernize a 1994 Chevrolet Impala; a purple 1969 Ford Mustang poses mechanical challenges; metal fabricator Kris presents an original hot rod.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: A 1954 Ford from the farm is transformed into a hot-rodding milk machine; a new Challenger receives retro updates; Will rides a vintage chopper.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: A client brings his powerful 1964 Chevrolet Malibu into the shop; the crew works on a 1973 'Cuda; Dave drives a 1972 Datsun.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: A white 1969 Chevrolet Camaro gets completely customized, while a green '69 Camaro purchased on the Internet becomes a bigger project than originally imagined.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The crew spends late nights and long hours preparing vehicles for AutoRama; an impeccable 1939 Buick is taken for a ride.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The crew spends a year restoring a 1939 GM Futurliner, an Art Deco-style bus that toured the nation as part of GM's Parade of Progress traveling exhibit.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The restoration of the 1939 GM Futurliner is completed; a Chevrolet Bel Air gets a new top; Dave recalls a Volkswagen Bus build.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: Dave and Kevin tackle a unique project; a 1967 Chevrolet Nova that was bought on the Internet needs a large amount of work.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The owner of 1960 Cadillac wants his vehicle transformed into a copper hot rod; a one-off 2010 Chevrolet Camaro requires repairs; the guys recall a 1972 Ford Bronco.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: Eighteen months of metal grinding and panel matching comes to an end as the Copper Caddy is readied for its big reveal.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: Dave comes up with a plan to transform a rusty '68 Chevelle plucked from the bowels of the shop; a 1955 Chevrolet Bel Air arrives for a chassis swap.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The team's newest member modifies the hood of a 2013 Mr. Norm's Challenger; a new rear suspension is installed on a 1948 Studebaker.

programme.desc: Dave has his hands full trying to locate and build a 1959 Buick Invicta; twin 2014 Audis looking for some styling cues.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The guys work on a rare 1952 Pontiac but find something lurking beneath the metal that threatens the whole build.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The team struggles to get the trim, paint and upholstery just right on the '52 Pontiac; custom graphics are added to a 2014 Challenger.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The owner of a 1969 Chevrolet Camaro has a very specific mission for Dave and the crew; the upholstery shop repairs a worn-out convertible top; body man Brandon brings his ride into the shop.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The shop builds a gasser race car, but Dave and the client disagree on a color for the vehicle; Dave readies a 2012 pickup truck for his teenage son, who had bad luck with his first car.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: Dave plans to redesign a 1955 Ford F-100 with a custom hourglass-shaped bed, extended cab and sleek grill, but the client has his own ideas for the interior.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The guys scramble to get the Ford F-150's exhaust, car color and engine just right; Dave hopes the owner of the super car farm truck will like the color he selected for the interior.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: An original FE engine, modified bumpers and some insights from Kevin make the revival of a 1957 Ford Fairlane a build to remember; a father and son challenge the team to make their engines roar.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The Fairlane build concludes, as the vehicle is bodyworked, painted, assembled and upholstered; getting the old-school engine fired and working properly proves to be a challenge.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: Dave and Kevin take the show on the road to Loveland, Colo., where they hope to capture a major award for the Copper Caddy and Mischief, a 1952 Pontiac; a 1966 Chevelle arrives for upholstery and an engine upgrade.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: A 1968 Chevrolet Camaro has a new owner who wants to finish off the build with a new front valance, rear spoiler and fresh paint; a 1965 Ford Mustang with mechanical issues gets an upgrade.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: Kindig-It Design finally finishes the 1967 Chevrolet Nova after 16 years of work; despite looking pretty stock on the outside, the vehicle boasts a unique power station underneath the hood.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: The guys set their sights on a major car show in Pleasanton, Calif., and work to get a Chevelle road ready for its owner.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: Dave goes out on a limb and purchases a 1929 roadster to fix up and flip, but Kevin fears he's bought another lemon; Kris tackles an engine that won't cooperate and hopes a two-dollar fix can solve a million-dollar problem.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides
programme.desc: Dave returns to his roots when he purchases a 1965 Volkswagen Bus to restore, reupholster and flip; the 1968 Boss Mustang arrives for some upgrades.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Full Throttle
programme.desc: JJ, Ryan, Murder Nova, Kye and Axman each build a car from the ground up, then meet up to put them to the test against each other and then against the world.

programme.title: Shorty's Dream Shop
programme.desc: Shorty tries to match the Ember Glow hemi's original color, then works his magic in the paint booth; Joel tries to figure out how to install an aftermarket AC; PG works on the interior; the crew is left with an enormous amount of trim.

programme.title: Shorty's Dream Shop
programme.desc: A big fan of the movie "Training Day" wants his '78 Monte Carlo to be built in the same vein; the G-body needs the works, including a swapped LS, beefed-up brakes and suspension and a new interior; Shorty's G10 parts runner van needs a facelift.

programme.title: Shorty's Dream Shop
programme.desc: Shorty's right-hand man in the body shop, Joe Gomez, has been in remission from cancer for nearly 10 years and wants to create a widebody Honda Accord for his wife to show his appreciation.

programme.title: Shorty's Dream Shop
programme.desc: After losing a grandson in a racetrack crash, Rick Wilson and his family want to raise money to promote safety at tracks around Texas; they enlist Shorty and the team to build a 1956 Corvette that will be auctioned off for their cause.

programme.desc: Still exhausted from SEMA in Las Vegas, Mark and the Ghoulz get back to the grind; Will prepares to paint his first 1970 AAR 'Cuda; with Mark breathing down his neck and micromanaging his methods, Will's rebellious nature is stirred up.

programme.title: Engine Masters
programme.desc: As far as dual-quad manifolds go, it's hard to beat the cool factor of the tunnel-ram and cross-ram setups; to see which makes more power, the guys bolt each to the 383 Chevy and stagger jets until their gasoline-soaked hearts are content.

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: The guys get their '73/74 dirt-track Duster back up and running for a road trip and off-road.

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: The guys attempt to keep a '39 Ford running on a 1,000-mile road trip.

programme.title: Garage Squad
programme.desc: A twist of fate reunites a man with a Dodge Li'l Red Express he once bought from his boss at a local pizza joint.

programme.title: Garage Squad
programme.desc: A 1965 Chevy C10 was the perfect father-son project until the father tragically lost his son; the truck has sat in the garage ever since, and now the Squad steps in to help turn a place of sadness into one of renewed joy.

programme.title: FantomWorks
programme.desc: A rental vehicle loaded with parts and a dream of a perfect Plymouth Business Coupe arrives at the shop; Dan works to locate a 1972 Buick Gran Sport and then restore it with hard-to-find features.

programme.title: FantomWorks

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike tracks down what he believes is the finest-handling front wheel drive car ever made: the Lotus Elan M100; if Ant can get the engine and suspension back to glory, then Mike is convinced that some cosmetic upgrades will seal the deal.

programme.title: Iron Resurrection
programme.desc: A client loves her trucks, particularly the 1950 Chevy, but with a list of customizations the size of Texas, this build is going to seriously test Joe's mechanical genius.

programme.title: Iron Resurrection
programme.desc: A 1935 Chevrolet Master rolls into the shop; made famous by the gangsters of its time, the vehicle features a distinctive grill, wood interior and "suicide doors.".

programme.title: Iron Resurrection
programme.desc: A weather-beaten 1959 Metro rolls into the shop for an extreme makeover from the inside out; the vehicle's new owner gets a surprise twist.

programme.title: Iron Resurrection

programme.desc: Mandi stumbles across a 1971 Volkswagen Karmann Ghia, and challenges her husband, Joe, to make the car cool.

programme.desc: Martin Bros. Customs prepares to transform a 1970 Chevy C10 into a powerful autocross hot rod; Shag and Amanda desperately search for a sturdy cab for the build.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides

programme.desc: Final details on the 1967 Pontiac GTO include tanzanite blue paint, redline wheels and a custom interior; a Chevelle arrives for a small but poignant upgrade.

programme.desc: Dave and the team at Kindig It Design start a new year with a one-of-a-kind build they have never attempted before, a 1964 Ranchero; the car looks to have had a lot of work done, but what's underneath the metal sets the team back.

programme.desc: The Ranchero build is an epic achievement for Dave and the whole Kindig It Design team, but they hope the client sees it the same way; Dave seeks the perfect name for the perfect car.

programme.desc: A Canadian father-daughter team arrives to add a new ride to their impressive collection; building the little sister to the '62 Safari Bus is something close to Dave's VW roots; fresh off the showroom floor, a 2017 Cadillac CTSV.

programme.desc: Dave and the team swap the chassis in a 1955 Nomad and upgrade its performance, which will give this car the edge it was missing.

programme.title: 'Twas the Text Before Christmas

programme.title: This Time Each Year

programme.desc: Abby, who has inherited the Haven Restaurant and Inn, is unable to manage both sides of the business, so she teams up with hot-shot chef Preston to shake up the holiday menu and get an investment from a restaurant mogul.

programme.desc: The nine baking pairs create a piece that shows their love for Harry Potter.

programme.title: Harry Potter: Wizards of Baking
programme.desc: The teams are in Gringotts Wizarding Bank, where James and Oliver Phelps ask the bakers to create 3-foot showpieces inspired by the bank's main hall, tunnels and vaults.

programme.title: Diagon Alley
